data.title: MainStreet Bancshares approves new $10M share buyback
data.description:
• MainStreet Bancshares (MNSB) Friday said its board approved a new stock repurchase program of up to $10M, replacing and superseding the buyback plan announced in October 2025.
  • The timing, size and price of any buybacks will be at management’s discretion, with no guarantee of purchases, the company said.

data.title: WSFS Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $1.68 beats by $0.18, revenue of $275.3M beats by $7.09M
data.description:
• WSFS Financial press release (WSFS): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.68 beats by $0.18.
  • Revenue of $275.3M (+7.5% Y/Y) beats by $7.09M.
  • At March 31, 2026, book value per share was $52.24, an increase of $0.97, or 2% (not annualized), from December 31, 2025.

data.title: Charter Communications' mixed Q1 results, broadband loss, weigh on shares
data.description:
Shares of Charter Communications (CHTR) are facing modest selling pressure on Friday morning following the release of mixed first-quarter results that included a decline in revenue and a larger-than-expected loss of broadband customers.

The parent company of Spectrum mobile and internet services added 368K new mobile lines (versus +432K estimates) during the quarter but lost 120K Spectrum Internet customers (versus -100K estimates) amid increased promotions from its competitors.

And although its video service lost 60K customers, it was less than the 85K decline analysts were expecting and compares to a loss of 181K customers in the same quarter last year. This can be attributed to Spectrum’s simplified pricing and packaging, along with the inclusion of programmers’ streaming applications in its expanded basic video packages.

For the first quarter, Charter Communications (CHTR) earned a profit of $9.17 per share in the first quarter, up 9% from a year ago but $0.91 below expectations. Adjusted EBITDA declined 2.2% to $5.6B, compressing adjusted EBITDA margin by 50 basis points to 41.5%.

On the revenue side, a 15.1% increase in mobile service revenue and advertising revenue was unable to fully compensate for losses in internet, video, voice, and residential service, all of which contributed to a 1.1% decline in revenue to a below-consensus $13.5B. 

The company’s balance sheet is also weighing on shares as free cash flow decreased 12.5% to $1.4B.

In response to the disappointing results, Charter Communications CEO Chris Winfrey assured investors that “as we continue to improve our products, pricing, packaging, and service, and complete our rural network initiatives, we are poised for improving customer and free cash flow growth.”

data.title: oint Stock Company Kaspi.kz raises $600M via oversubscribed 2031 bond issuance
data.description:
• Joint Stock Company Kaspi.kz (KSPI) issued $600M in 5.900% senior unsecured notes due 2031.
  • The bond offering was 3.5x oversubscribed with demand from around 130 institutional investors.
  • Proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes and to strengthen liquidity.
  • Coupon is set at 5.900%, with semi-annual payments starting October 28, 2026, and settlement on April 28, 2026.

data.title: AMD upgraded at DA Davidson on CPU explosion
data.description:
AMD (AMD) was upgraded at D.A. Davidson on Friday, as the investment firm cited a “structural increase” in CPU demand, due to agentic artificial intelligence.

Shares of the Dr. Lisa Su-led AMD rose more than 10% in premarket trading, following stronger-than-expected results from Intel (INTC).

“We are upgrading shares of AMD to Buy from Neutral and raising our price target to $375 from $220 based on a structural increase in CPU demand on top of much improved visibility into AMD's role in the great data center buildout,” analyst Gil Luria wrote in a note to clients. “We believe that given the magnitude of Intel's beat, there is meaningful upside to AMD's estimates, starting with the March quarter which AMD is due to report May 5th.”

Delving deeper, Luria said that he expects CPUs to be a bottleneck, given how important they have become to agentic AI, but Intel's report indicated that is not likely to be the case.

“We view Intel's results as a precursor for a huge step-up for AMD's CPU franchise and believe the structural shift towards agentic AI workloads is creating unprecedented demand for server CPUs," Luria added. "We believe AMD is favorably positioned to significantly raise prices across the portfolio to support and expand margins given our view that demand will outpace supply for the foreseeable future.”

data.title: Moog Non-GAAP EPS of $2.64 beats by $0.28, revenue of $1.05B beats by $20M
data.description:
• Moog press release (MOG.A): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.64 beats by $0.28.

data.title: First Hawaiian GAAP EPS of $0.55 beats by $0.01, revenue of $220M misses by $0.73M
data.description:
• First Hawaiian press release (FHB): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.55 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $220M (+4.3% Y/Y) misses by $0.73M.

data.title: China to restrict US funding in local tech firms after Meta's Manus deal: report
data.description:
Chinese regulators intend to curb technology companies, including some major AI pioneers, from accepting U.S. capital without government approval, part of China's broader response to Meta Platforms' (META) acquisition of startup Manus, Bloomberg News reported.

In recent weeks, agencies including the National Development and Reform Commission told several private firms they should reject capital of U.S. origin in funding rounds unless explicitly approved, the report added, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Moonshot AI, which is considering an IPO, was among those that received the guidance from the state planner. Another Chinese startup StepFun got similar instructions, the report noted.

Regulators have also decided on similar restrictions for TikTok-parent ByteDance (BDNCE). The regulators do not want the Beijing-based company, which also operates popular AI chatbots, to approve secondary share sales to U.S. investors without government approval, the report added.

ByteDance did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

The new curbs are broadly aimed at preventing U.S. investors from taking stakes in sensitive sectors where national security is a priority, according to the report.

The move follows Meta's acquisition of Manus, which was announced in December last year. Following the announcement, Beijing reportedly started reviewing the $2B acquisition earlier this year. Last month, Chinese authorities reportedly barred two Manus co-founders from leaving the country.

The commission — a powerful state planning agency with broad policy-making powers — is now heading a multi-agency investigation which includes the Ministry of Commerce, into the deal and its repercussions.

The new curbs also follow China's decision to restrict "red chips" — a type of Chinese company incorporated overseas — from seeking IPOs in Hong Kong, threatening a measure used by Chinese companies to access foreign capital by floating overseas, the report added.

The twin moves indicate that regulators are worried about a leakage of homegrown technology abroad as Chinese-founded startups and companies explore global opportunities. Following the Manus acquisition, many academics decried the loss of a valuable asset to the U.S. Many were concerned that the deal would encourage other startups to follow suit, the report noted.

At the core of the post-Manus debate was the way the startup restructured to make a sale to a foreign company possible before any regulatory review in China, the report added. Manus was a Singaporean-incorporated firm, but its founders hailed from China.

The U.S. has restricted investments into some Chinese technology sectors on concerns of helping advance its military or economic might. In 2025, U.S. rules designed to curb investment in Chinese-owned semiconductor, quantum, and AI companies took effect, the report added.

data.title: Norfolk Southern Non-GAAP EPS of $2.65 beats by $0.16, revenue of $3B in-line
data.description:
• Norfolk Southern press release (NSC): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.65 beats by $0.16.
  • Revenue of $3B in-line (flat Y/Y).
  • Railway operating revenues of $3.0 billion, up $5 million, or flat compared to the first quarter 2025, on a volume decline of 1% year-over-year.
  • Operating ratio in the quarter was 70.7% compared to 61.7% in first quarter 2025.

data.title: ALT5 Sigma, Nano Labs sign MOU to explore AI data centers, payments tie-up
data.description:
• ALT5 Sigma (ALTS) on Friday said it entered a non-binding memorandum of understanding with Nano Labs (NA) to explore collaboration on AI data centers, agent cloud infrastructure, and AI-native payments.
  • ALT5 said the agreement establishes a 90-day evaluation process led by a joint working group that will assess technical integration, commercial opportunities, and operational feasibility.
  • Companies will also review tokenization models, infrastructure monetization opportunities, and broader security requirements tied to AI and computing environments.
  • ALT5  provides digital asset trading and payments infrastructure, while Nano Labs focuses on high-performance computing, chip design, and data center systems.
  • The memorandum is non-binding, and any future partnership would require definitive agreements after the review process.
  • ALT5 shares are up +5% in premarket trading after settling 7.6% lower in the last close.
  • Nano Labs is down -3% before the bell, after a 9% jump in Thursday session.
